attacking_style: Launches decisive long-range solo attacks 15-50km from finish on penultimate or final categorized climbs, preferring sustained power efforts over explosive accelerations. Will attack multiple times in final 800m-1km if initial move fails, particularly when hunting time bonuses.
team_tactics: UAE sets tempo 6km from finish to isolate him, but he frequently operates alone after domestiques are exhausted. Refuses cooperative work when equal or stronger than companions. Personally hunts intermediate sprint bonuses rather than relying on teammates.
terrain_preference: Dominates on sustained 10-15km climbs with technical descents to follow (demonstrated across Lombardia, Tour stages, Tirreno). Excels on 20%+ gravel gradients. Less explosive on short, steep walls like Mur de Huy. Shows altitude/accumulation weakness on first high-mountain efforts in Grand Tours.
vulnerability: Crumbles on first high-altitude summit finishes of Grand Tours (lost 2:51 stage 11 TDF 2022, struggled 2020 Dauphiné). Crashes with frequency in positioning/technical sections. Predictable attack patterns allow rivals like Vingegaard to mark closely. Team depth insufficient for back-to-back mountain stages against deeper squads.
key_patterns: Attacks 15-50km out on penultimate climbs, building 30-90 second gaps through solo power (8+ observations)
Hunts every available time bonus aggressively: intermediate sprints, finale kicks, even on flat stages (6+ observations)
Refuses cooperation when stronger, tactically withholds work to preserve sprint advantage in reduced groups (5+ observations)
Cramping/physical crisis management: survived cramps at 12km Lombardia 2023, broke on altitude debuts multiple years
Won debut Grand Tour at 21 by waiting until stage 20 ITT; repeats late-strike pattern across stage races
Descends recklessly/aggressively to extend climbing gaps, treating technical sections as acceleration zones (4+ observations)
form_trajectory: positive: Despite 2023 Tour loss, won 3rd consecutive Lombardia while managing cramps, took Flanders/Amstel/Fleche triple, and showed sprint power even after 3-week deficit. 50%+ 2023 win rate indicates sustained dominance outside Vingegaard matchups.
built_from: 2022 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
2023 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
2023 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
